perturbation.original_text: Oceanic Time Warner Cable will include the following disclaimer on the Watchit Content: "The materials shown are for entertainment purposes only and does not provide any guarantees of winning or improving your odds of winning on this program. The rules of the games as shown may not apply to all properties and may change from time to time."
perturbation.changed_text: Oceanic Time Warner Cable may, at its discretion, include a disclaimer on the Watchit Content.
perturbation.explanation: This change makes the disclaimer optional rather than mandatory. This introduces a legal risk because disclaimers related to gaming/gambling content are often legally required to protect consumers from misleading or deceptive practices. Removing the mandatory aspect could violate consumer protection laws regarding fair advertising and disclosure, potentially making Time Warner liable for misleading content.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 15 U.S. Code § 52 - False advertisements; liability of media
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification makes the disclaimer optional. 15 U.S.C. § 52 addresses false advertising. If the 'Watchit Content' contains misleading information about improving odds of winning, and the disclaimer is not included, Oceanic Time Warner Cable could be held liable under this law. The original clause mandated the disclaimer to mitigate this risk; the modified version removes this protection.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 15 U.S. Code § 52 - Dissemination of false advertisements | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 15 U.S. Code § 52 - Dissemination of false advertisements U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next (a) Unlawfulness It shall be unlawful for any person, partnership, or corporation to disseminate, or cause to be disseminated, any false advertisement — (1) By United States mails, or in or having an effect upon commerce , by any means, for the purpose of inducing, or which is likely to induce, directly or indirectly the purchase of food, drugs, devices, services, or cosmetics; or (2) By any means, for the purpose of inducing, or which is likely to induce, directly or indirectly, the purchase in or having an effect upon commerce , of food, drugs, devices, services, or cosmetics. (b) Unfair or deceptive act or practice The dissemination or the causing to be disseminated of any false advertisement within the provisions of subsection (a) of this section shall be an unfair or deceptive act or practice in or affecting commerce within the meaning of section 45 of this title . (Sept. 26, 1914, ch. 311, § 12, as added Mar. 21, 1938, ch. 49, § 4, 52 Stat. 114 ; amended Pub. L. 93–637, title II, § 201(c) , Jan. 4, 1975 , 88 Stat. 2193 ; Pub. perturbation.justification: The original contract mandated a disclaimer on the 'Watchit Content,' while the modified version makes it optional. 15 U.S.C. § 52 explicitly addresses the 'dissemination of false advertisements,' making it unlawful to disseminate any false advertisement 'for the purpose of inducing, or which is likely to induce, directly or indirectly the purchase of food, drugs, devices, services, or cosmetics.' By making the disclaimer optional, Oceanic Time Warner Cable risks violating this law if the content is misleading. The original mandatory disclaimer mitigated this risk, which is now removed, creating a direct contradiction with the aim of the law to prevent false advertising.
